chunk_summary: Guidance on energy density and texture safety in complementary feeding for infants aged 6–12 months, emphasizing meal frequency, spoon test consistency, and choking prevention strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of energy density in complementary feeding for infants aged 6–12 months.
Learn how to assess food texture using the spoon test and ensure safe progression to finger foods.
Identify choking hazards and implement safety measures during feeding.
Apply WHO meal frequency guidelines for infants aged 6–12 months.
key_facts: WHO recommends 2–3 meals/day for 6–8 months and 3–4 meals/day for 9–11 months, with snacks as needed.
Complementary foods should pass the spoon test: thick enough to sit on a spoon without dripping.
Fat provides 9 kcal/g; adding 1 teaspoon (5 g) of oil or ghee contributes ~45 kcal to meals.
Thin liquids like dal water or rice water fail the spoon test and may lead to poor weight gain.
Choking hazards include whole nuts, seeds, grapes, popcorn, and sticky candies; foods should be cut into safe shapes.
reference_quote: WHO guidance supports 6–8 months 2–3 meals/day and 9–11 months 3–4 meals/day, with nutritious snacks added as needed and continued breastfeeding.
document_key_topics: Texture safety and progression for infants
WHO meal frequency guidelines for infants
